nodes.title: Neg
widgets_values: shoes, boots, Overexposure, blurred, subtitles, paintings, poorly drawn hands/faces, deformed limbs, cluttered background, oversaturated, (text, watermark:1.3)

Loading ref images

This area allows you to load 1 or 2 images to use as a ref image for the video.

Options for each ref

Crop subject
- this can crop out the subject you mask, allowing you to better combine 2 ref images together.
Remove background
- This places the subject on white allowing you to prompt the background and so it does not conflict with the video background.
- Mask select you choose the mask you want to crop out.



Combine subjects
This allows you to combine 2 subjects into one image allowing you to make videos with 2 people.
This can swap the left/right or even up/down.  Use this to best place the subjects so you get the most detail you can.
Use pad or crop here to set the ref image to proper size

Video copy source

This loads a video to copy.  This loads batches.

Options

Load the video by right clicking on any video in windows, select copy as path.  Then paste that in the video loader, no need to remove quotes.

Can change the FPS, I recommend you do all, 24-30 fps... but you can half the frames and do 12-15.  The quality of copy is better at high fps but its double the work.


Processing size
- This sizes for the processing of controls.  1280 usually good, larger takes longer to process, but can offer better quality movement.

Render size
- This just sets the size you will be rendering the video at.  This sets the largest side of the video to this number and scales other side to this.

Frames per batch.
- Set this to something wan likes multiple of 4 plus 1. (49,65,81,101,153)
- This sets how many frames will be loaded for each batch.  
- 49-153 has worked.  You can set this best for your video card.

Its best to look at the total frames(in the loader) and divide it by the number of batches you want to split it into, then choose a frames per batch one level higher than that number using the multiple of 4+1.  The above wanimagetovideo node has the correct sizes and frame counts you should use.

I have added auto batching because i got sick of mathing every video.
Auto/manual batching (use one of these for the whole video)

- If auto batching is enabled it will grab your frames per batch for the batch set in batch number.  Increasing this number grabs the next batch.
- auto batching you cant change the frames per batch between batches.

- Manual start was added because sometimes you dont want the same number in each batch.
- The start frame is where the video will start from and grab your frames per batch.  You can manually change this between batches, but you need to math it out.

Saving

I dont auto save, i dont save every video i make only ones i want to keep.

I right click on the video and open preview, then save to where i want.

Compression

H264 works
hevc is better

Save the color matched video below if extending and want it matched.

Video Controls

Here are the main controls used to copy the video.
All controls can be used alone or combine with others.  Combining others take care to adjust strengths as full strength on multiple can cause video burning.

All these can have resolution changed, higher is better copy and harder to change, lower is worse copy and easier to change.

Choices
Pidnet
- The secret sauce of wan 2.1 vace that many dont use.  A true middle ground between depth and openpose.
- This copies movement nearly as well as depth, and allows subject change almost as well as openpose.
- its simple, fast and works for 80% of things people want to do.
- it can fail badly when the video has too many strait lines and upside down flips etc.

Depth
- This is used when you NEED to copy movement exactly, (spins, flips, body contortions)
- This limits how much you can change the subject
- this can be added at strength 0.4-0.6 to help other controls 
- this can be combined with a black image to lower the strength of the depth control.
-- setting 0.3-0.9 (higher is weaker depth) on the lower depth node can allow more changes to the subject while still keeping most movement.

Openpose
- I rarely use this but many people like it.
- This allows full change of subject, but no context of background.
- Prompting background and prompting in general is more important here.

Canny
- This COPIES.  Hard copies.  Best used if you want to change the style of a video and not the subjects. 


Strengths of each can be changed.  This also holds the ref image so careful going too low when using just one.

You can make the first video with one control and change for the next batch, or add.  The first batch has no start image its hardest.  The start image is powerful, it FORCES the video to start properly.  This can allow you to make a start video with pidnet, then change to depth for second batch for better movement copy for complex things but still have the pidnet guide to help depth change the subject easier.

nodes.title: Pos
widgets_values: a realistic and detailed video of a woman alone in her backyard on a patio flipping and kicking, arched back, head back

Video extending

This loads a video (previous batch renders) and sets the start frames of the new video to its end frames.

This automaticly adds the start frames to all controls if enabled and generates masks needed.

You choose the guild frames to load, this number is added to your frames per batch.  Setting 4-8-12-16 is best to keep frame counts good for wan.  You can change this number for each batch if some need less or more guides.

4 guides are usually fine because of the controls HOWEVER.  This has issues with memory between batches.  It only knows whats in the guide frames.  If you have backgrounds that are covered in the guide frames, it wont know whats behind them.  This means backgrounds can change if the subject is moving.  Adding more guides can help to allow it to see behind the subject as they move.
--- THis happens alot when a subject is in the start and end but not the middle.  It forgets what the person looks like if the batch guides dont contain the info.  Happend with man/woman gymnastics where the man goes out of frame for the middle and bg changes, it forgets the man and the background from the start.

You only need to load the previous video into the loader the same as the original.  

Both masks and guide frames will be shown.

2 extra options when extending

Color matching
- can use a color mathching node set to color match the new video to the video being extended.  Sometimes this helps a lot, other times it makes things worse.

Test merge videos.
- This can merge the generated video with the extended video.
- I did this for testing, if doing more than one extend, its best to save each video alone and combine them all after because if you save the combine each time the video compression can build up. 


-----------------------------------------------------------------------
More advanced versions of this flow include frame memories.  You can insert 1 or 2 images (frames containing info not in the last frames of the extend video) to the start 

and another flow that allows full start and end videos alowing merging two videos and allowing you to start in the center of a video and extend both forward and backward minimizing contrast drift on long batches.

Both of these are a mess and no one but me is following the mess so when i have time i will fix them up to put out.

Quick start guide

Ensure all models and loras are selected, reselect all to ensure you have proper locations for yours.

Load a ref image to the top image loader of the subject you wish to use

Load a video into the video loading and batching group
 - Change batch size to your chosen size(49, 65, 81, 101, 153)
 - ensure batch number is 1 for first batch
 - set size to something you can handle (384, 512, 640, 848)

Enable pidnet(or a chosen control video)

Set prompt to what you need for the video

Ensure all extend options disabled for first batch

run the flow.


Extending is the same but you just need to enable the extend and load the video to extend and change batch number up one.


My tip is every video make a folder
in that folder put the source video
load the source and when saving save all batches to this folder, and name them 1,2,3,4,5 etc.  

When loading the extends then you only need to change the number when moving to the next batch.  and now all batches are numbered.

Video renders

Here we have some info and the prompts

Prompting is important, if something is wrong, prompt it.  If something is not in the ref image, prompt it.

Adding colors can help, hair styles, clothing. 

DO NOT PROMPT FACE FEATURES. eye color, freckles, even emotions like laughing will change the face.  You will force the text to video to change the ref image face.

Wan NAG is enabled, meaning the negative works.  You can bypass the nag node to speed up processing a bit but negative will be ignored.

Negative can help remove things from video that show up you dont want.

CHANGE SEED BETWEEN BATCHES

Render

One sampler, 

defaults
8 steps
cfg 1
eular a
simple (or beta57)


Steps can be 4-12  (4 works but hair/hands suffer, 8 is good, 12 is overkill)
Eular a works, LCM, ddim, unpc, many others work but IMO most contrast shift more than eular a or LCM.

Beta57 is from a node set, it can help some videos.
It allows slightly more high noise allowing more change but can cause movement problems with complex things.

Simple is good normally

Model Loading

This uses vace 14b.  1.3b can work but not all controls work properly.  The quality of 1.3b is good...

Can load regular models or GGUF.  I prefer Q8 GGUF over all.

Can load vace module like wan wrapper can into a text to video 14b.


Loras can be loaded if wanted here, can change between batches.


Lightx2v used as speed up lora
Many choices, 
i found the rank 32 to work best for base video
I found rank 256 to work best to extend with less contrast shift.

The adaptive works decent but have not tested it much, i think it takes longer to render with it.

1.0 strength for speed up loras IMO but can work lowering them for extends.

VAE - Best used is FP32.  FP16 works fine.


PUSA lora works to help spins, flips, upside down, complex movements etc.

Ultimate simplified wan 2.1 vace
I took out many things most would not understand and confuse everyone... This can still do a lot.  If enough people like this flow i can fix up the full ones that fixes many of the issues with this flow but it would take time and i dont know if its worth it with the pace of LTX taking over.  LTX cant do some things like skipping, or flips but its really good at everything else.

This is not fast, its a slow, and difficult process to make this work for the things i show... I have done this a LONG time and know how to manipliate the flow to get results, I spent 8 hours to 4 days for each video... Multiple takes, multiple batches...  On a 3060. This is why i never understood the hate i have recieved :P. I worked harder to make the ai copy than the people making the originals usually. I now use a 5060 and 2.1 vace still takes ages.

This flow was designed to be used with my face fix inpaint flow that fixes faces.
Faces in this output can be .... not great.  Vace face inpaint is actually VERY good.  

Going further than 4 batches you will notice a significant drift in contrast. This is worse with a static camera, anything static on the screen will drift and not look good after 6 batches.  Moving cameras work well because it wont over contrast the areas remaining the same.  PIDnet copies background movement.

That being said nothing copies like vace... yet.

This workflow is designed to simplify video to video copies with wan 2.1 vace

This workflow uses 3 main tricks that many vace flows dont use that i have been using for a a year.

Tricks used

Multiple control options
- All controls can be combined, or used alone, many ways to control their strengths.

Pidnet lineart controls
- This is the main control used, allowing better control than openpose, and better subject change than depth.  This is the secret sauce of vace that changes the game.

Video extend features
- You can load a video to extend allowing you to resume videos allowing longer generations in batch form.  


Will be putting out the face fixing flow that works just like this allowing face inpainting but again... i make messes with my flows.
